summary: coral-wraith postinstall steals AWS credentials and source code to hardcoded C2
details: On npm install, postinstall.js gates execution on a 12-hex-character hostname (the default Docker container hostname pattern) and, when matched, performs a multi-stage credential and source-code theft against the installer's environment. It shells out to dump /app source files and git history, greps for HTB-style flag patterns, then requests an IMDSv2 token from 169.254.169.254 and harvests the IAM role's AccessKeyId/SecretAccessKey/Token. Using those credentials it runs `aws secretsmanager list-secrets` and `aws secretsmanager get-secret-value` to dump Secrets Manager contents. The collected output is chunked and PUT to two hardcoded HTTP endpoints — http://127.0.0.1:30782 and http://154.57.164.71:30782 — disguised inside a fake 'ecto_module' YAML manifest. The hostname gate is targeting/evasion logic to hide the payload from developer machines and only fire inside container victims.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
